medications.indication_rationale: Should not be stopped abruptly without medical advice given its likely cardiovascular indication; management focus should be urgent bleeding control rather than medication cessation decisions in this acute call.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Lean forward (not tilt the head back), breathe through the mouth, and spit out rather than swallow blood while arranging urgent transport.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces aspiration risk and swallowed blood causing nausea/vomiting while awaiting definitive treatment.

patient_education_points.point: Because firm pressure for 45 minutes hasn't stopped the bleeding, and blood is running down your throat, you need urgent in-person treatment to pack or cauterise the bleeding vessel — this can't be fixed at home now.
Please go to the Emergency Department now for this.
Lean forward, breathe through your mouth, and spit out any blood rather than swallowing it.
Don't stop your aspirin or blood pressure tablets on your own — let the treating doctors advise on that.
If you feel severe dizziness, chest pain, or the bleeding gets even heavier, call 000 immediately.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Bleeding control method (packing/cautery), haemoglobin level, and medication review regarding aspirin
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Severe dizziness, chest pain, or worsening bleeding — call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Correct positioning advice (lean forward, breathe through mouth, spit not swallow)
Advice not to stop aspirin without medical guidance
errors_of_commission: Advising the patient to tilt the head back
Advising immediate cessation of aspirin without medical guidance
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Failing to recognise that first aid has already failed and recommend urgent in-person care is a significant safety failure.
